title: xerid aird middle-eastern vegetables pilaf
description: a light middle-eastern pilaf dish featuring spicy gochugaru and a sweet glaze, easy to make and packed with flavor. it's a great vegetarian option for a quick lunch or dinner

ingredients.name: pomegranate molasses
ingredients.notes: available at middle-eastern grocery stores or online

instructions: heat the olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
add the chopped bell pepper and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, until tender.
add the gochugaru, flour, salt, black pepper, and sumac, and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
add the mixed rice and cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly, until the rice is lightly toasted.
add the pomegranate molasses and water, and bring to a boil.
reduce the heat to low and cover the saucepan with a tight-fitting lid.
simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until the liquid has been absorbed and the rice is tender.
fluff the pilaf with a fork and stir in the chopped parsley and pine nuts.
heat the glaze over low heat, until warm and runny.
drizzle the glaze over the pilaf and serve.
garnish with additional parsley and pine nuts, if desired.
